skill.name: reference-finder
skill.description: Automatically finds and ranks PubMed references for each sentence in scientific text. Use when you need titles, DOIs, and brief recommendation reasons from the PubMed E-utilities API.
skill.summary: Finds and ranks PubMed references for each sentence in your scientific text automatically.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 48 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 3 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings flagged documentation text mentioning the PubMed API and an audit results JSON file containing no cryptographic operations. The 23 'shell backtick execution' findings flagged markdown code blocks showing example CLI usage in documentation files. The 7 'system reconnaissance' findings flagged standard evaluation comments and dictionary operations. The 13 'network' findings flagged legitimate PubMed E-utilities API endpoints (eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov) and standard library HTTP calls. The skill is a legitimate research tool that queries the official PubMed API for literature references. No prompt injection attempts were detected. Risk level is low because the skill makes network requests to a trusted external API.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at scripts/find_refs.py:8 and SKILL.md:3. Both locations contain documentation text mentioning 'API' in the context of describing the skill. No cryptographic operations exist in the codebase.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Shell Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged backtick characters in SKILL.md and evaluation-checklist.md as code execution. These are markdown code blocks and inline code formatting showing example CLI usage or documenting API URLs. No actual shell execution exists in the Python code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged dictionary operations and evaluation text as system reconnaissance. The flagged lines contain standard Python dictionary checks (pmid lookup deduplication) and evaluation comments in the audit results JSON.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Network Access to PubMed API
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to the official PubMed E-utilities API using Python standard library urllib. All connections target eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov. This is required for the skill's core function and not a security concern.

content.user_title: Find PubMed References for Your Scientific Text
content.value_statement: This skill finds relevant PubMed references for each sentence in your scientific text. It saves time by searching, ranking, and recommending the best literature matches with titles, DOIs, and reasons.
actual_capabilities: Splits scientific text into individual sentences for targeted reference searches
Searches PubMed using extracted biomedical keywords from each sentence
Returns top 3 ranked references per sentence with title, DOI, PMID, and year
Provides a short recommendation reason for each suggested reference
Supports both direct Python API calls and command-line interface usage
Ranks results by keyword match strength and publication recency
limitations: Requires internet access to reach the PubMed E-utilities API
Limited to PubMed database only and does not search other literature databases
Does not retrieve full abstracts or citation counts for references
Cannot access paywalled or subscription-only content

use_cases.title: Manuscript Literature Review
use_cases.description: A researcher drafts a scientific paper and needs to verify each claim has supporting citations from PubMed.

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Preparation
use_cases.description: A principal investigator writes a grant application and needs recent references to support each research aim.

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Screening
use_cases.description: A reviewer processes a collection of scientific abstracts and needs automated PubMed reference suggestions for each abstract.
use_cases.target_user: Systematic review teams and medical librarians

prompt_templates.title: Basic Single Sentence Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Find PubMed references for this scientific text: CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing has revolutionized biomedical research.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a single sentence lookup

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Sentence Paragraph Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this paragraph and find references for each sentence: mRNA vaccines represent a breakthrough in vaccine technology. They offer rapid development timelines compared to traditional approaches. Clinical trials show high efficacy rates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing a full paragraph with multiple claims to verify

prompt_templates.title: Custom Number of Reference Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the top 5 PubMed references for this text about cancer immunotherapy and checkpoint inhibitors. Return results with titles, DOIs, and recommendation reasons.
prompt_templates.scenario: Requesting more than the default 3 references per sentence

prompt_templates.title: Batch Research Abstract Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Process each sentence in this research abstract and provide PubMed reference suggestions with titles and DOI links for every claim: [paste your abstract text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Systematic processing of longer scientific texts for comprehensive literature grounding

output_examples.input: CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing has revolutionized biomedical research.
output_examples.output:
Found 3 relevant references:
1. A Programmable Dual-RNA-Guided DNA Endonuclease in Vitro (2012)
   DOI: 10.1126/science.1225829
   PMID: 22745249
   Reason: keywords 'CRISPR-Cas9' High degree of matching

2. Multiplex Genome Engineering Using CRISPR/Cas Systems (2013)
   DOI: 10.1126/science.1231143
   PMID: 23287718
   Reason: keywords 'CRISPR-Cas9' High degree of matching

3. Genome editing with CRISPR-Cas9 (2015)
   DOI: 10.1016/j.cell.2015.05.052
   PMID: 26046660
   Reason: keywords 'CRISPR-Cas9' High degree of matching

output_examples.input: mRNA vaccines offer rapid development timelines compared to traditional vaccine approaches.
output_examples.output:
Found 3 relevant references:
1. mRNA vaccines - a new era in vaccinology (2018)
   DOI: 10.1038/nrd.2017.243
   PMID: 29416095
   Reason: keywords 'mRNA' High degree of matching

2. mRNA-based therapeutics - developing a new class of drugs (2014)
   DOI: 10.1038/nrd4278
   PMID: 25212520
   Reason: keywords 'mRNA' High degree of matching

best_practices: Use specific biomedical terminology in your input text for better keyword extraction and more relevant reference results
Review the suggested references manually before including them in academic work or publications
Verify DOI and PMID values against PubMed directly when using references for critical citations
anti_patterns: Do not use vague or general language - the keyword extractor needs domain-specific terminology for good results
Do not expect references for non-biomedical topics - the tool only queries the PubMed database
Do not bypass the 0.5 second request interval between API calls to respect PubMed rate limits

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses the PubMed public E-utilities API which does not require an API key or authentication.

faq.question: What databases does this skill search for references?
faq.answer: It searches only the PubMed database through the official NCBI E-utilities API endpoints.

faq.question: How many references are returned per sentence?
faq.answer: The default setting returns 3 references per sentence. You can change this with the max_refs parameter.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without internet access?
faq.answer: No. The skill needs internet access to communicate with the PubMed API and retrieve reference data.

faq.question: Does the skill retrieve full text of articles?
faq.answer: No. It retrieves metadata only including title, DOI, PMID, and publication year for each reference.

faq.question: What type of text works best with this skill?
faq.answer: Scientific text with domain-specific biomedical terminology produces the best keyword extraction and reference matching.
